revision.text:
[[ja:布制神社]] {{Expand Japanese|布制神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Fuse Shrine (Q85880047) | native_name = 布制神社 | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Fuse Shrine (Q85880047) | coordinates = {{coord|36.579388888888886|138.13330555555555}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=布制神社.jpg }} {{nihongo|'''Fuse Shrine'''|布制神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Shinano Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} * Province|ja|信濃国の式内社一覧|qid=Q11386164|1=List of Shikinaisha in Shinano Province}} ** Series ordinal: 9 ** P155: {{ill|Q85879982|ltq=Q85879982|qid=Q85879982|1=Kawaai Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q135039918|ltq=Q135039918|qid=Q135039918|1=Haheshinano Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Nagano (city)|lt=Nagano|af|Nagano|an|Nagano|ar|ناغانو (ناغانو)|arz|ناجانو|ast|Nagano|azb|ناقانو (شهر)|az|Naqano|ban|Nagano, Nagano|ba|Нагано|be|Нагана|bg|Нагано (град)|bn|নাগানো|br|Nagano|ca|Nagano|ceb|Nagano Shi|ce|Нагано|cs|Nagano|da|Nagano|de|Nagano|devoyage|Nagano|el|Ναγκάνο|en|Nagano (city)|envoyage|Nagano|eo|Nagano|eovoyage|Nagano (urbo)|es|Nagano|et|Nagano|eu|Nagano|fa|ناگانو (شهر)|fi|Nagano|fivoyage|Nagano|fo|Nagano|fr|Nagano|frvoyage|Nagano|fy|Nagano (stêd)|ga|Nagano|gl|Nagano|he|נאגנו|hr|Nagano|hu|Nagano|hy|Նագանո|id|Nagano|idvoyage|Nagano|ie|Nagano|io|Nagano|it|Nagano|ja|長野市|javoyage|長野市|ka|ნაგანო (ქალაქი)|kk|Нагано|ko|나가노시|ky|Нагано|lt|Naganas|lv|Nagano|mdf|Нагана (ошсь)|mn|Нагано|mr|नागानो|mzn|ناگانو (شهر)|nl|Nagano (stad)|nn|Nagano|no|Nagano|os|Нагано|pcd|Nagano|pl|Nagano|plvoyage|Nagano|pt|Nagano (cidade)|ptvoyage|Nagano|qu|Nagano|ro|Nagano|ru|Нагано|runews|Категория:Нагано|sco|Nagano, Nagano|sc|Nagano|sh|Nagano|simple|Nagano (city)|sk|Nagano|sl|Nagano|sr|Нагано|srnews|Категорија:Нагано|sv|Nagano|sw|Nagano, Nagano|szl|Nagano|tg|Нагано|th|นางาโนะ (เมือง)|tl|Lungsod ng Nagano|tr|Nagano|tt|Нагано|uk|Нагано|ur|ناگانو، ناگانو|uz|Nagano|vi|Nagano (thành phố)|war|Nagano (syudad)|wuu|长野市|zh_classical|長野市|zh_min_nan|Nagano-chhī|zh_yue|長野市|zh|長野市|qid=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}} * {{ill|Shinano Province|lt=Shinano Province|ckb|پارێزگای شینانۆ|cs|Provincie Šinano|de|Provinz Shinano|en|Shinano Province|eo|Provinco Ŝinano|es|Provincia de Shinano|fa|ولایت شینانو|fr|Province de Shinano|gl|Provincia de Shinano|hak|Shinano-koet|id|Provinsi Shinano|it|Shinano (provincia)|ja|信濃国|ko|시나노국|lt|Šinano žemė|ms|Wilayah Shinano|nl|Shinano (provincie)|pl|Prowincja Shinano|pt|Província de Shinano|ru|Синано (провинция)|simple|Shinano Province|sk|Šinano (provincia)|sr|Провинција Шинано|th|แคว้นชินาโนะ|tr|Shinano (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Сінано|zh_min_nan|Sinano-kok|zh_yue|信濃國|zh|信濃國|qid=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}} * {{ill|Q7423207|ltq=Q7423207|qid=Q7423207|1=Sarashina District, Nagano}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|36.579388888888886|138.13330555555555}} * {{coord|36.579445|138.133293}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181977 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Fuseno Shrine|lt=Fuseno Shrine|qid=Q135039917|1=Fuseno Shrine (Q135039917)}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '長野県長野市篠ノ井布施五明', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:布制神社]] {{wikidata link|Q85880047}} == Japanese content == {{Source stated|date=November 2025}} {{Shrine |Name = Fuse Shrine |Image = [[File:Fuse Shrine.jpg|250px]]
Torii gate and worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|qid=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|qid=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}} 235 Shinonoi Fusegomei |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|Q11435420|qid=Q11435420|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} (disputed) |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = |Alternative name = |Annual festival = Last Sunday of September |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Nagano|label=Fuse Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Fuse Shrine''' (ふせじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Fusegomei Shimo-roku-tan, Shinonoi, {{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|qid=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|qid=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. In addition to this shrine (Shinonoi Fusegomei), there are two other shrines with the same name within the same area ({{ill|lt=Fuse Shrine (Shinonoi Ishikawa, Nagano City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinonoi Ishikawa}}, {{ill|lt=Fuse Shrine (Shinonoi Yamabuse, Nagano City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinonoi Yamabuse}}). The enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|Q11435420|qid=Q11435420|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto}}, with associated deities {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|qid=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime-Ōkami|1=Toyouke-hime}}, {{ill|qid=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|Q9150391|qid=Q9150391|qid=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata}}, and {{ill|lt=Yasaka-tome-no-kami|Q9150392|qid=Q9150392|lt=Yasaka Tome-no-Mikoto|1=Yasakatome}}. Within the precincts, in addition to the heiden (offering hall), there are a mikoshi hall, various small shrines, and the enshrined divine spirit of {{ill|lt=Kitano Tenman-gū|Q662176|qid=Q662176|1=Kitano Tenmangū}}, among others. === Overview === This shrine enshrines {{ill|lt=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|Q11435420|qid=Q11435420|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto}}. The date of its founding is ancient; it is listed in the “{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}” (927), the oldest extant record of official shrines in Japan, but the exact year of establishment is unknown. The previous shrine buildings collapsed due to the {{ill|lt=Zenkoji Earthquake|Q11418893|qid=Q11418893|1=1847 Zenkoji earthquake}}, and were rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Kaei|Q1146125|qid=Q1146125|1=Kaei}} 4 (1851). Due to deterioration, a complete reconstruction was carried out in September of {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|qid=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 15 (2003), resulting in the current buildings. At the innermost part of the shrine complex is the main hall, built in {{ill|lt=Bunka|Q11042|qid=Q11042|1=Culture}} 6 (1809) in the nagare-zukuri style of a one-bay shrine, richly decorated with carvings themed on {{ill|lt=kachōfūgetsu|Q11615743|qid=Q11615743|1=kachōfūgetsu}} (flowers, birds, wind, and moon). ・{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fuse Honshō|1=UNKNOWN}} is said to have been the name of this shrine’s estate. ・Toward the end of the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, local youths attempted to loot the {{ill|lt=Senryū Shōgunzuka Kofun|Q17222794|qid=Q17222794|1=Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun}}. Astonished by the quantity of buried items, they reported the matter to the {{ill|lt=Matsushiro Domain|Q903575|qid=Q903575|1=Matsushiro Domain}}. Following the domain’s decision that the excavated items be deposited at this shrine, they were preserved as the shrine’s treasures. Some were later transferred to the {{ill|lt=Nagano City Museum|Q11654184|qid=Q11654184|1=Nagano City Museum}}. === Shrine precincts === Within the precincts are the mikoshi hall, Ten’ō Shrine (Tsushima Shrine), Sericulture Shrine, Akiba Shrine, Konpira Shrine, Kōtaijingū, Yama-no-Kami Shrine, Tenman Shrine, Iizuna Shrine, Tarō Shrine, Jijū Shrine, and the shrine of Kitano Tenman-gū. * Mikoshi Hall A palace-style mikoshi used during the Gion Festival is housed here. The festival began to commemorate Fuse Village’s elevation to town status as Shinonoi Town in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|qid=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1914|Q2083|qid=Q2083|1=1914}}). The Gion Festival is held on the last Saturday and Sunday of July. On the Sunday one week prior, a ritual called “Ten’ō-oroshi” is performed, in which the sacred object of the Ten’ō Shrine (Tsushima Shrine) enshrined within the precincts is placed on a mikoshi and transferred to a temporary shrine set up along Shinonoi Station Street. On the final day, the “Ten’ō-age” rite is conducted, after which the mikoshi is returned to the Mikoshi Hall. * Ten’ō Shrine (Tsushima Shrine) – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}. * Sericulture Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Uke Mochi|Q2280323|qid=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}}. * Akiba Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Hi-no-Kagutsuchi|UNKNOWN|lt=Hi-no-Kagutsuchi-no-Kami}} (Kagutsuchi). * Konpira Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto / Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}}. * Kōtaijingū – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}. * Yama-no-Kami Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|qid=Q386563|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}}. * Tenman Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|qid=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. * Iizuna Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke-Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} (Toyouke-bime). * Tarō Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōgetsuhime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}. * Jijū Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Jijū-Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}}. * Kitano Tenman-gū Heiden – The divided spirit of {{ill|lt=Kitano Tenman-gū|Q662176|qid=Q662176|1=Kitano Tenmangū}} in Kamigyō Ward, Kyoto Prefecture, was transferred and enshrined on September 5, Heisei 15 (2003). === Location === 235 Shinonoi Fusegomei, Nagano City, Nagano Prefecture === Access === About a 10-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Shinonoi Station|Q5357193|qid=Q5357193|1=Shinonoi Station}} on {{ill|lt=East Japan Railway Company|Q499071|qid=Q499071|lt=JR East|1=East Japan Railway Company}} and {{ill|lt=Shinano Railway|Q11254401|qid=Q11254401|1=Shinano Railway}} lines === Sources === * Ministry of Doctrine (ed.), *Tokusen Shinmeichō*, Isobe Kōyōdō, 1925. National Diet Library Digital Collection info:ndljp/pid/1919019 * Ōta Ryō, *Shinano*, Isobe Kōyōdō, Taishō 13. National Diet Library Digital Collection https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/982146 (accessed 2025-05-23) * Muraoka Ryōsuke, *Kōshin Kitei*, vol. 2, Asano Rihei, Meiji 43.1. National Diet Library Digital Collection https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/765021 (accessed 2025-05-23) === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Nagano Prefecture}} {{Shinto navigation}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Fuse Shrine (Q85880047)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Fuse Shrine (Q85880047)]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nagano (city)]] [[Category:長野市の歴史]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]]
